title: coccocypselum spicatum
intro: Coccocypselum spicatum, also known as the Wild Lime or Wild Orange, is a shrub-like plant native to Central and South America that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties due to the presence of essential oils like limonene and beta-pinene. The health benefits of Coccocypselum spicatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
